directives.title: Initiate UI Rearchitecture Brainstorming with Ora Skill
steps: Invoke the 'Ora superpowers library, brainstorming skill'.

Provide the following high-level design request as input: 'Change the current two-panel app layout to a three-panel layout. The new rightmost panel should function as a separate command-style window. This window must support loading additional context such as competitor videos, PDFs, resources, and viral Twitter threads. The user interaction within this panel should include planning videos, reviewing plans, and tracking associated to-dos.'
Engage interactively with the brainstorming skill by answering clarifying questions regarding intent, requirements, and design until a comprehensive understanding is reached.
Upon completion of the brainstorming session, instruct the skill to generate and save a detailed plan document reflecting the discussed design.
directives.rationale: This directive outlines a concrete interaction with a named skill, providing specific input (a detailed prompt for UI changes), describing an interactive phase (Q&A), and specifying a tangible output (a plan document).

directives.title: Transfer Brainstorming Output to External Planning System
steps: Retrieve the 'implementation summary' or plan document generated by the Ora brainstorming skill.
Pass the content of this implementation summary document as input context to the designated external planning system (e.g., 'claude codes builtin plan mode').
directives.rationale: This directive provides a clear, two-step procedure for data transfer between distinct system components, specifying both the source and destination of the information.

directives.title: Create an Isolated Git Work Tree
steps: Invoke the 'git worktree skill' to initialize a new isolated work environment for the current project.
Confirm the creation of a '.worktrees' directory in the project's root.
Verify that the newly created work tree within '.worktrees' contains a complete clone of the project's state at the time of creation.
directives.rationale: The transcript explicitly instructs to 'call this git worktree skill' and clearly describes the direct, verifiable outcomes of this action, providing enough detail for an autonomous agent to perform the action and validate its success.

directives.title: Run Verscel's React Best Practices Audit
steps: Navigate to the root directory of the Next.js project.
Execute the 'Verscel's React best practices' skill/tool.
Review the generated audit report for performance optimization recommendations.
directives.rationale: The transcript explicitly instructs to 'pop down into the project and actually run this thing' and states that 'this skill audited my entire project against those best practices'. This provides a clear, executable action to run a named tool/skill against the current project for an audit purpose.

directives.title: Run Codebase Static Security Analysis
steps: Invoke the static analysis tool/plugin on the project codebase.
Analyze the codebase against known security vulnerabilities.
directives.rationale: The directive explicitly commands to 'run this static analysis of our codebase' with the clear objective to 'analyze our project against known security vulnerabilities', which is a concrete, executable task for an autonomous agent with access to such a tool.

directives.title: Implement API Rate Limiting
steps: Design a rate limiting strategy for all project endpoints.
Implement the chosen rate limiting mechanisms (e.g., per IP, per user, per endpoint).
Apply these rate limiting mechanisms to all public-facing API endpoints within the project to prevent abuse and ensure system stability.
directives.target: our project endpoints (API gateway, backend services, or specific route handlers)
directives.rationale: The transcript identifies a critical missing feature ('missing rate limiting') and provides a clear imperative ('we need to have rate limiting systems in place') with a defined purpose ('abuse the out of the endpoints'). This is a standard security implementation task.

directives.title: Analyze and Critique App Design System using Agent Browser Skill
steps: Instruct the agent to activate the 'agent browser skill'.
Direct the agent to analyze the project's visual components and overall design.
Direct the agent to critique the implementation of the app's design system.
Ensure the critique is conducted against known design system best practices.
directives.target: my project, our app's design system (frontend application, UI components, design system documentation, possibly a running instance of the app)
directives.rationale: This directive is a direct command given to an agent: 'ask the agent to use the agent browser skill to analyze my project and critique the implementation of our app's design system...against known best practices.' This specifies the tool, action, target, and criteria.

directives.title: Critique App Design System Implementation
steps: Instruct the agent to utilize the 'agent browser skill'.
Command the agent to analyze the current project.
Direct the agent to critique the implementation of the app's design system.
Ensure the critique evaluates the implementation against both the app's specified design system and known best practices.
directives.target: App's Design System Implementation (within the current project)
directives.rationale: This directive provides a clear, sequential set of instructions for an autonomous agent, specifying the tool to use ('agent browser skill'), the subject of the analysis ('current project' and 'app's design system'), and the criteria for evaluation ('app's design system and known best practices'). The expected output is a critique or audit.
